quality.rationale: 多步张量推导结合投影算子性质+3+1分解核心几何恒等式

origin.question: Problem 9.30. Prove that the extrinsic curvature of a timelike hypersurface with unit normal vector $\mathbf{n}$ is $-\frac{1}{2} \mathcal{L}_{\mathbf{n}} \mathbf{P}_{\alpha \beta}$ , where $\mathbf{P}_{\alpha \beta} = \mathbf{g}_{\alpha \beta} - \mathbf{n}_{\alpha} \mathbf{n}_{\beta}$ is the projection tensor into the hypersurface.
origin.solution: Solution 9.30. From the definition of the Lie derivative (Problem 8.13) we have$$\begin{array}{l} \mathcal {L}_{\mathbf {n}} \mathrm {P}_{\alpha \beta} = \mathrm {P}_{\alpha \beta ; \gamma} \mathrm {n}^{\gamma} + \mathrm {P}_{\gamma \beta} \mathrm {n}^{\gamma}; \alpha + \mathrm {P}_{\alpha \gamma} \mathrm {n}^{\gamma}; \beta \\ = \left(\mathrm {g}_{\alpha \beta} - \mathrm {n}_{\alpha} \mathrm {n}_{\beta}\right); \gamma \mathrm {n}^{\gamma} + \left(\mathrm {g}_{\gamma \beta} - \mathrm {n}_{\gamma} \mathrm {n}_{\beta}\right) \mathrm {n}^{\gamma}; \alpha + \left(\mathrm {g}_{\alpha \gamma} - \mathrm {n}_{\alpha} \mathrm {n}_{\gamma}\right) \mathrm {n}^{\gamma}; \beta \\ = - \left(\mathrm {n}_{\alpha} \mathrm {n}_{\beta}\right); \gamma \mathrm {n}^{\gamma} + \mathrm {n}_{\beta}; \alpha + \mathrm {n}_{\alpha}; \beta \tag {1} \\ \end{array}$$where we have used $\mathfrak{n}_{\gamma}\mathfrak{n}^{\gamma};a = \frac{1}{2} (\mathfrak{n}_{\gamma}\mathfrak{n}^{\gamma});a = 0.$ If $\mathbf{e}_i$ are three basis vectors in the hypersurface, then$$\mathrm {K}_{\mathrm {i j}} = - \mathrm {e}_{\mathrm {j}} \cdot \nabla_{\mathrm {i}} \mathrm {n} = - \mathrm {n}_{\mathrm {i}; \mathrm {j}} = - \frac {1}{2} \left(\mathrm {n}_{\mathrm {i}; \mathrm {j}} + \mathrm {n}_{\mathrm {j}; \mathrm {i}}\right) \tag {2}$$where we have used the fact that $\mathbf{K}_{ij}$ is symmetric. If we are using a general coordinate system (i.e. not one in which $i, j$ lie in the hypersurface), then Equation (2) can be rewritten$$\mathrm {K}_{\alpha \beta} = - \frac {1}{2} \left(\mathrm {n}_{\gamma ; \delta} + \mathrm {n}_{\delta ; \gamma}\right) \mathrm {P}_{\alpha}^{\gamma} \mathrm {P}_{\beta}^{\delta} \tag {3}$$i.e. as the symmetrized covariant derivative of $\mathbf{n}$ projected into the hypersurface. Using the explicit form of $\mathbf{P}_a^\gamma$ in Equation (3), and comparing with Equation (1), we find$$\mathrm {K}_{\alpha \beta} = - \frac {1}{2} \mathcal {L}_{\mathbf {n}} \mathrm {P}_{\alpha \beta}.$$
